EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line, preceding a cold front, crossed east-central Florida during the afternoon. A bow echo along the leading edge of the squall line moved rapidly east across southern Orange County. A swath of winds 45-60 mph accompanied the bow echo and produced tree damage along its path. A book-end vortex rapidly evolved at the northern end of the echo, and resulted in an EF-0 tornado within the corridor of straight-line winds. EVENT_NARRATIVE: A book-end vortex rapidly developed at the northern edge of a bow echo. A NWS Storm Survey confirmed an EF-0 tornado, with estimated ground-level winds of 65-75 mph. The tornado developed at the east edge of Lake Mary Jane (possibly beginning as a waterspout over the lake) and was embedded within a larger region of strong straight-line winds. The tornado impacted three blocks of homes in the Isle of Pines subdivision, then continued into a rural, unpopulated area farther east/northeast. While the homes sustained little direct structural damage from the winds, falling trees damaged several homes and vehicles along the tornado path. Winds at tree top level (50-100 feet above ground level) were estimated at 80-90 mph. Velocity data from the TMCO Orlando International Airport TDWR radar showed winds up of 100 mph at 300 feet above ground level. DI28 DOD 3 LB; DI2 DOD 1 EXP.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front crossed the Florida Peninsula on the evening of the 17th and morning of the 18th, with a band of showers and thunderstorms developing ahead of the front.  Some of the stronger storms produced damaging wind gusts during the afternoon of the 17th as the storms came onshore.  Additionally, one person was injured from a rip current on Siesta Key.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media relayed a report of a power pole down on a car on Forest Land Drive in Tampa, FL, near the intersection of Gunn Highway and Anderson Road.  Time was estimated by radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong cold front crossed the Florida Peninsula on the evening of the 17th and morning of the 18th, with a band of showers and thunderstorms developing ahead of the front.  Some of the stronger storms produced damaging wind gusts during the afternoon of the 17th as the storms came onshore.  Additionally, one person was injured from a rip current on Siesta Key.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A 19 year old teenager was swimming in waste deep water when he was pulled out past the buoy line by a rip current and pulled under the water. He was rescued by a friend and needed to be resuscitated by CPR.  The victim was then taken to a hospital but is expected to make a full recovery.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A band of thunderstorms developed ahead of a cold front that approached West Central Florida on the afternoon of the 29th. Some of the stronger storms produced damaging wind gusts, hail of up to one inch, and heavy rain.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media relayed a report of a tree down across Shoal Line Road and Cortez Boulevard.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level trough over the southeast U.S. along with a cold front in central Florida caused scattered showers and thunderstorms across south Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An amateur radio operator reported branches and signs down at the intersection of Jog Road and Hypoluxo Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Onshore flow between high pressure to the north and low pressure offshore of the Florida Atlantic Coast brought waves of heavy rainfall onshore.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Elevated river levels combined with heavy rainfall caused flooding in the Haw Creek Preserve State Park area. The cost of damage was unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal line of storms, some severe, tracked acrosss SE Ga and the Suwannee River Valley of NE Fl during the afternoon. The line decreased in coverage and intensity over northeast Florida during the early evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were blown down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall in and mesolow tracked across southeast Georgia and northeast Florida in several waters of convection through the day. High instability was present, with upper level forcing in place ahead of a long wave trough. The tornado was a associated with the core of a mesolow that tracked ENE inland from the Gulf Coast and over Columbia county and NE over Charlton County. Very heavy rainfall of 4-6 inches occurred over 24 hours with hourly rainfall rates of 2-3 inches in some locations.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Eddy Tower RAWS station measured 4.55 inches in 24 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered thunderstorms developed along sea breeze boundaries during the afternoon hours. A couple of the storms caused damaging wind gusts that knocked down tree branches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several large tree limbs were reported to be snapped near the intersection of South Himes Avenue and West Morrison Avenue.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level trough of low pressure and its associated cold front moved across South Florida producing a squall line with a pronounced bow echo feature that raced across the area with strong wind gusts and isolated severe thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The Delray Beach Fire Department estimated thunderstorm wind gusts up to 65 mph. They also reported that several tree brances fell on townhome causing minor damage.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A squall line moved across northwest Florida during the morning hours of March 16th.  Although no reports of severe weather were received, lightning did damage a home.  In addition, heavy rainfall of 3-5 inches caused some rivers and streams to come out of their banks with areal flooding reported in some areas.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Widespread heavy rainfall of 3 to 5 inches over a 12 to 24 hour period led to local creeks and streams rising out of their banks and producing areal flooding across numerous county roads and neighborhood roads.  Several county maintained roads were washed out or had culverts damaged.  Public works repaired or replaced at least 6 culverts and positioned 100 sandbags to divert water.  Affected roads included Mt. Gilead Church Rd, Bernard Johnson Rd, Blue Bar Rd, Calvin Morgan Rd, River Rd NE, Preston Sheffield Rd, Glennis Cruce Rd, and Dekle Beach Rd.  Jody Morgan Rd was closed due to two damaged culverts.  The county has repaired 6 Culverts at $7730 total with 10 load lime rock at $1000 total.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A stalled frontal boundary produced localized areas of heavy rain which caused minor flooding in low-lying areas as well as along some area rivers.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding of several manufactured homes begins on 32nd and 33rd street in Ruskin when the gauge on the Little Manatee River at Wimauma reaches 14.5 feet.  Moderate flooding continued until October 3 and the maximum crest was 14.67 ft. About 10 other homes were cutoff as roads were inundated.  This area floods one to five times a year and residents move vehicles and other valuables out of the area when flooding is expected.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level trough and surface cold front moved through the region, bringing a round of severe weather across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A roof was damaged by strong winds.  Monetary damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A sharp upper level trough propelled a cold front through the Florida peninsula, fueling a squall line.  Some of these storms produced damaging wind gusts.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Sumter County Emergency Management relayed a report of a tree and power lines down on a mobile home on Legion Road in Wildwood.  Time was estimated based off radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon sea breeze boundary collisions combined with cool air aloft, allowed for the development of severe thunderstorms that produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A local newspaper relayed a report of a lightning strike to a home in the Villages. The home sustained substantial damage. No one was home when the strike occurred and no injuries or fatalities resulted.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level low pressure area over the eastern Gulf of Mexico caused scattered showers and thunderstorms across South Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A funnel cloud was reported near Royal Palm Beach at the intersection of State Road 7 and Okeechobee Blvd.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cut-off low pressure area formed over the southeast states producing scattered showers and thunderstorms over South Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Broadcast media reported a groundskeeper was struck and injured by a lightning strike in Coral Gables. The 56-year-old man was found unconscious and in cardiac arrest and a defibrillator was used to restore his pulse and breathing. He had burn marks on his clothes but was responsive and talking upon arrival at the hospital.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon sea breeze boundary collisions combined with cool air aloft, allowed for the development of severe thunderstorms that produced large hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A caller reported Ping Pong Ball sized hail off of Caraway Drive in Poinciana.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong long wave trough was centered over the mid Mississippi Valley extending south to the Gulf coast with several shortwaves rotating around it.  During the April 29-30 time period, a strong MCS developed along the Gulf coast and trained across the area, producing extremely heavy rainfall rates and amounts on top of what had already been a very wet month with additional rainfall amounts of 6 to 10 inches common across the area.  This led to serious flooding across portions of the Florida panhandle with several million dollars in damage reported, mainly to roads.  However, several structures were also impacted by flood waters, especially in Walton county, and a FEMA disaster declaration was obtained across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Approximately 64 roads were closed or underwater across Jackson county due to heavy rain and flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong storm system brought record flooding along with severe thunderstorms that produced damaging winds and tornadoes to the region.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A roof was blown off of a house at Duke Airfield just south of Crestview. Numerous trees were reported down.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Periods of widespread heavy rainfall impacted northeast Florida ahead of an approaching cold front and low pressure system that developed over north central Florida.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Observer measured 4.87 in 24 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong pre-frontal squall line crossed the area late morning through mid afternoon. Strong wind shear and helicity promoted rotation cells as well as bowing line segments. One comma-head storm formed along the Interstate 10 corridor near Live Oak, and this cell raced eastward toward Jacksonville. The storm had a long-lived mesocylone, and the rear flank downdraft descended downward several times causing brief periods of strong low level rotation which caused a few tornado touchdowns. The RFD produced strong winds of 50-80 mph and caused widespread tree damage and some structural damage as the comma-head raced across north Florida, including the Jacksonville metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: The roof of an apartment complex was damaged at 127 South 1st Avenue. The time of damage was based on radar and timing of wind gusts in the area from observation stations.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A passing short wave trough combined with a lingering surface trough and sea breezes produced strong to severe storms during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: At least 8 trees were blown down across northern Columbia county generally north of Lake City. The time of the damage was based on dispatch center call logs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough moved slowly across the southeast states. A couple of rounds of moderate to heavy rainfall spread across the area in association with it. The ground was already saturated given heavy rain earlier in the month. As a result, significant flash flooding and areal flooding resulted.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Waits Street was impassible due to flash flooding.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong pre-frontal squall line crossed the area late morning through mid afternoon. Strong wind shear and helicity promoted rotation cells as well as bowing line segments. One comma-head storm formed along the Interstate 10 corridor near Live Oak, and this cell raced eastward toward Jacksonville. The storm had a long-lived mesocylone, and the rear flank downdraft descended downward several times causing brief periods of strong low level rotation which caused a few tornado touchdowns. The RFD produced strong winds of 50-80 mph and caused widespread tree damage and some structural damage as the comma-head raced across north Florida, including the Jacksonville metro area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A home was damaged from thunderstorm winds at 5750 SW 61st Ave. in Jasper. The extent of damage was unknown.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak low level trough extended across the Okefenokee Swamp southward across the Suwanee River Valley, which triggered early morning slow moving showers and thunderstorms across Columbia county. A moisture feed off of the Gulf of Mexico due to low level SSW winds and spokes of energy rotating around a low center meandering over the local area fueled slow moving convection, which caused flash flooding over Columbia county generally between the cities of Columbia and Lake City. Daily storm total amounts ranged from 7 to almost 10.5 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: An observer measured 10.38 inches in 24 hours.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Deep tropical moisture was across the area with PWAT of 2.1 inches with deep layer WSW flow. A surface front meandered over SE Georgia. Slow storm motion of 8 kts favored slow storm motion, and multiple forcing mechanisms spawned widespread rainfall covered.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Daily rainfall of 3.25 was measured by a COOP-Observer.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An unseasonably strong upper level trough and cold brought a round of strong to severe thunderstorms and heavy rainfall to the area.  There were several tornadoes confirmed across northwest Florida.  All of the tornadoes were rated EF0, and most of them occurred in forested areas with no structures impacted.  However, there was some minor damage to structures around Cottondale in Jackson county and Sumatra in Liberty county.  All of the tornadoes exhibited tornadic debris signatures using dual-pol radar.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding was reported on Appleyard Drive with road blocks in place.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Heavy rains from a stalled frontal boundary in late September caused moderate flooding along the Little Manatee River near Wimauma from September 28 until October 3.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Flooding of several manufactured homes begins on 32nd and 3rd street in Ruskin when the gauge on the Little Manatee River at Wimauma reaches 14.5 feet. The maximum crest was 14.67 ft. About 10 other homes were cutoff as roads were inundated. This area floods one to five times a year and residents move vehicles and other valuables out of the area when flooding is expected.  Damage of $30,000 was reported in September as moderate flooding began on the 28th.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A deep tropical moisture plume was draped across South Florida ahead of a stationary cold front over North Florida resulting in scattered afternoon thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public relayed a report of a brief funnel cloud via social media in Palm Beach Gardens.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A passing short wave trough combined with a lingering surface trough and sea breezes produced strong to severe storms during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter observed penny size hail.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The sea breezes combined with lingering outflow boundaries triggered scattered afternoon thunderstorms which mainly affect the NE Florida coastal counties. Cold temperatures aloft around -11 to -12 deg C combined with high instability yield a cluster of severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A downburst with winds estimated at 60-65 mph leveled numerous trees.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A very moist and unstable airmass combined with sea breezes and a passing upper level short wave trough sparked scattered strong to severe storms over the area during the afternoon and evening hours. Precipitable water values were 2-2.2 inches.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter estimated wind gusts to 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line moved across northeast Florida during the afternoon and early evening hours. A few strong to severe storms produced wind damage, hail as well as funnel clouds.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Lightning struck a home in Julington Creek which damaged the fireplace and removed tile from the fireplace wall. The cost of damage was estimated for the event to be included in Storm Data and the estimated cost of damage is likely inaccurate.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A cold front over southeast Georgia combined with the sea breezes and cold upper level temperatures of -14 deg C at 500 mb all phased over the area to produce scattered severe thunderstorms during the afternoon and evening.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were blown down throughout Gainesville. Reports including trees of 15-20 inches in diameter blown down at NW 21st Avenue and 77th Street, a 2 ft diameter tree down across SW 35th Place, a tree down on a vehicle one SW 13th Street, tree down at NW 13th Terrace and NW 6th Place, tree down at 7300 Lakeshore Drive and a 12-15 inch diameter tree down at SW 8th Place and SW 60th Terrace, and a 12 inch diameter tree down on SE 15th Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A pre-frontal squall line of severe storms tracked across SE Ga during the morning hours and then across NE Fl during the early to mid afternoon hours. Severe storms produced widespread wind damage with a few isolated rotating supercells. Sufficient heating near the St. Johns river basin produced elevated surface based instability during the afternoon, and updrafts were greatly enhanced by this forcing. A few isolated pre-squall line cells blossomed over Putnam...St. Johns and Flagler counties in the afternoon and produced large hail. A wall cloud was observed with a rotating storm over Putnam county, and a tornado warning was issued for this cell, however, ground based tornadic damage was not reported.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A spotter reported winds of 55-60 mph along I-95 West to NW of the St. Augustine area.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A passing upper level disturbance combined with elevated instability produced a few severe storms along the northern periphery of a warm front located from Flagler county westward to Marion and Alachua county in the early afternoon.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Nickel size hail was observed on Red Oak Lane.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak upper level troughing in the area contributed to significant instability during the afternoon hours with enhanced coverage of strong to severe storms across the area.  Damaging wind was the main impact with these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Trees, tree limbs, and power lines were blown down around town.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Weak upper level troughing in the area contributed to significant instability during the afternoon hours with enhanced coverage of strong to severe storms across the area.  Damaging wind was the main impact with these storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Multiple trees were blown down on Fred George Road at Mission Road and Old Bainbridge Road.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A ridge of high pressure north of Florida led to moderate easterly gradient flow and a sea breeze convergence along the western half of the Florida peninsula. Scattered thunderstorms developed, producing hail and damaging wind gusts throughout the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Social media reports were received of nickel sized hail near Westchase.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Scattered showers and thunderstorms developed during the afternoon, with one storm producing a brief lived EF0 tornado. The tornado damaged 10-12 houses before dissipating.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tornado briefly touched down in the Colonial Hills neighborhood of Riverview, damaging 10 to 12 houses, mostly with roof and window damage. Several homes had 10 to 20% of the asphalt shingles stripped off, and one home was missing a section of plywood roofing as well. An anchored outbuilding was completely destroyed and thrown into a home, significantly damaging the concrete block walls of the home. Several boats were flipped and thrown off of trailers and/or moved 10-15 yards, and one was lifted to a nearly 90 degree angle up into a tree. Additionally, fences and pool enclosures were completely destroyed, and several large trees were uprooted or sustained damage to large limbs.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed ahead of a strong cold front and produced high winds which caused damage across the western Florida panhandle. Heavy rain also caused flooding.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Winds estimated at 80 mph caused damage to fences and a utility shed.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A brief EF-0 tornado touched down in Lee County causing minor roof damage to a home. The tornado then moved into the water and continued as a waterspout for a few minutes before dissipating.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A brief EF-0 tornado touched down in Lee County causing minor roof damage to a home. The tornado then moved into the water and continued as a waterspout for a few minutes before dissipating.||Damage to the home was consistent with EF-0 damage - namely a few roof shingles ripped off. Palm fronds still present on the palm trees. Winds estimated between 45-55 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe storms broke out across NE Fl during the late afternoon and evening hours as the sea breezes interacted on the south side of a diffuse frontal zone over an unstable airmass. Also, a passing upper level short wave trough enhanced thunderstorm severity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees and power lines were blown down along State Road 19 near Silver Lake Drive. The time of damage was based on radar.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Severe storms broke out across NE Fl during the late afternoon and evening hours as the sea breezes interacted on the south side of a diffuse frontal zone over an unstable airmass. Also, a passing upper level short wave trough enhanced thunderstorm severity.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A possible tornado touchdown was reported near Newberry. Trees and power lines were blown down by storng wind gusts. One person was injured in an apartment complex.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A large upper level trough covering the eastern U.S. caused numerous strong thunderstorms over the east coast Metro area of South Florida with isolated severe storms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Photos were received via social media depicting several large trees uprooted along Johnson Street.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A strong upper level trough and surface cold front moved through the region, bringing a round of severe weather across the area.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Numerous trees were snapped off at the trunk, and two large pine trees knocked down power lines.  A carport was also demolished.  Monetary damage was estimated.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Thunderstorms developed over the interior and produced locally damaging winds along with quarter sized hail.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: Several large trees were downed in the town of Lake Helen by winds estimated at 60 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: A weak upper level trough of low pressure along with a weak stationary frontal boundary over South Florida generated numerous afternoon showers and thunderstorms.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A member of the public reported quarter size hail 2 miles south of Sawgrass Mills Mall.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: Afternoon sea breeze collisions caused strong to severe storms to develop across interior sections of the state. One thunderstorm produced quarter size hail in Lake Wales.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A picture of dime to quarter size hail was sent in by the public near Lake Wales.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: An upper level trough of low pressure and its associated strong cold front moved across South Florida causing an isolated severe thunderstorm to develop over northeast Miami-Dade County and move into southern Broward County.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A trained spotter relayed through Social Media that several tree branches were down in Cooper City with an estimated wind gust of 61 mph.

EPISODE_NARRATIVE: The typical scattered afternoon convection in summertime resulted in a few severe storms with trees and power lines blown down.
EVENT_NARRATIVE: A tree was blown down onto State Highway 146.
